steps.dialogue: Welcome back! It's great to see you and to be able to learn some more things about fractions with you today. Let's first take a look back at what you've accomplished so far.
We've been working with unit fractions - those fractions that show one part, like [fraction numerator=1, denominator=4]one fourth[/fraction]. Here's a quick reminder of what they look like...

[fraction numerator=1, denominator=2]one half[/fraction]    [fraction numerator=1, denominator=3]one third[/fraction]    [fraction numerator=1, denominator=4]one fourth[/fraction]    [fraction numerator=1, denominator=6]one sixth[/fraction]

Today, we're going to learn something new about fractions. What if you want to show MORE than one part? What if you want two parts, or three parts, or even all the parts? That's what we'll explore - how to BUILD bigger fractions by adding unit fractions together.
We're ready to build on unit fractions. Today we'll discover how to combine them to make larger fractions. Let's explore this next step together!
